  "instructions":"1) Write your names and index number on the answer booklet as they appear on your registration form and DO NOT write your names and index number on additional answer sheets of paper if provided. 2) Do not open this paper until you are told to do so, 3) This paper consists of three sections A, B and C Section A: Answer ALL questions. (55 marks) Section B: Answer THREE questions. (30 marks) SECTION C: This section is compulsory. (15 marks) 4) Use only a blue or black pen.",
   "answerKey":{
      "1":"c) Red blood cells",
      "2":"a) Temperature regulation through sweating",
      "3":"a) Lactic acid",
      "4":"d) Amphibians have scales",
      "5":"d) Supports body",
      "6":"Method Type Spermicide Barrier accept also Non-Hormonal Condom Barrier Implant Hormonal IUD Non-hormonal also accept hormonal or barrier Pills Hormonal",
      "7":"a) False b) True c) True d) True e) False f) True",
      "8":"The process of meiosis produces unique reproductive cells called gametes which have half the number of chromosomes as the parent cell. During fertilization the fusion of gametes from two individuals, restores the diploid condition. Gametes are haploid (containing only one set of chromosomes) while the zygote is diploid (containing two sets of chromosomes). Thus, sexually reproducing organisms alternate between haploid and diploid stages. In sexual reproduction, two individuals produce diploid offsprings that have characteristics from both parents. The gametes are produced from parent cells via the process called meiosis.",
      "9":"Blood component function White blood cells Defend the body against pathogens Red blood cells transport of respiratory gases platelets Clotting of blood",
      "10":"a) Yes b) Yes c) No",
      "11":"a) Testis / testicle / male gonads b) Ovary /Ovaries / female gonads",
      "12":"a) D: 3-year-old boy. b) Milk contains important proteins which are very essential for the growth/Body building /development and repair of the body. Children have growing bodies than adults. Hence they are given more milk than grandfather who is adult. c) A 28 year old mother needs extra iron because:- Formation of more blood in both foetus and mother.- Formation of haemoglobin- Formation of red blood cells- Avoid anaemia-Transport of more oxygen",
      "13":"Glucose is a simple sugar/monosaccharide which can be broken down easily to produce energy. The Absorption of glucose is also very fast in the blood.- No need of its digestion",
      "14":"Protection/ keeping proper hygiene Keeping personal hygiene by washing your hands often with soap and clean water Using sanitizer, cleaning and disinfecting surfaces that are often touched Cover the food in cleaned utensils and avoid drinking non boiled water.",
      "15":"To allow cross pollination to take place casily. To ensure high yield production To ensure support each other against wind To allow maximum absorption of water/ Easy irrigation To maximize land use",
      "16":"Report to authorities, parents, teachers and guardians. Help the victim to avoid to be isolated Giving the victim medical assistance in case sexual assault is attempted/to take the victim to the ISANGE ONE STOP CENTER /Hospital / Health center. Avoiding peer pressure Counseling Avoid drugs abuse Education/ sensitization Consider legal actions Say no Gathering and keeping physical evidences",
      "17":"Industrial effluent should be treated for safe use on the land that is used as manure or for disposal, Industrial plants treat their waste water before dumping in water bodies to prevent pollution. For safe use on the land that is used as manure or for disposal To prevent pollution To prevent eutrophication Avoid acidification of water bodies Prevent water-borne diseases Production of biogas Avoid death of aquatic organisms For being reused.",
      "18":"a) Antigens bind at the active /antibody/ antigen-binding/binding site forming antigen-antibody complexes. b) Passive / active / adaptive immunity is a naturally acquired immunity.",
      "19":"a) Take the patient immediately to the hospital for treatment, Give the person rehydration salts/fuids Give the patient the Intravenous fluids and electrolyte. b) Daily practices to ensure good health include: i) Brushing our teeth three times in a day and every after a meal. ii) Practicing moderate physical exercises iii) Healthy eating: balanced diet, avoid too much fats/eating at close intervals iv) Vaccination and immunisation practices v) Regular medical check-up in hospitals vi) Sanitation i.e keeping our surroundings clean.",
      "20":"Monoculture crops have no genetic variability because they are not allowed to reproduce naturally. This exposes their pests and weeds to environmental adaptations and become resistant to chemicals used to control them as the only way to control pests in this set up is to use more chemical control mechanisms Susceptibility to pests: The pests and weeds that attack them adapt easily to the environment and develop resistance to chemicals used to control them. Environment pollution through the use of dangerous synthetic chemicals/ Eutrophication May cause eutrophication that affect aquatic life Use of fuel energy in combination with the chemical fertilizers and pesticides, cause environment pollution (Air, soil and water) and lead to climate change May cause habitat destruction through searching enough space for growing crops for rising demand for food and other agricultural products Extinction of some species Soil erosion Use of dangerous synthetic chemicals It leads to soil degradation Drought Extreme weather condition Use of biofuels Conflict Food wastage",
      "21":"a) Liquid part of blood b) Plasma transports blood cells, nutrients such as glucose and amino acids; transports antibodies, metabolic waste products... Transportation/ distribution of blood cells Transportation/ distribution of antibodies Transportation/ distribution of waste substances such as carbon dioxide and urea Transportation/ distribution of Hormones such as adrenaline, insulin and glucagon c) White blood cell: Protects the body against microorganisms. d) A or B or AB or O e) +/- factor: Rhesus factor or Rh factor f) For proper blood transfusion, to avoid incompatibility of blood/agglutination/ in case of blood transfusion / For Safe organ transplant. g) Platelets are responsible of blood clotting.",
      "22":"a) Genotypes of the parents: AO and BO or I4 I° and I° b) Phenotype of parents: group A x group B Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I° Gametes: I*, I° and I*, I° The genotypes of their children are: 25% or = AB 25% or > BO 25% or AO 25% or + OO Or Phenotype of parents: group A x group B Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I° Gametes: I*, I° and I*, I° The genotypes of their children are: 25% or KB 25% or ~ I8 I° 25% or IA I° 10",
      "23":"a) Mitosis: is a type of cell division in which a parent cell divides itself into two identical daughter cells which are genetically the same as the parent cell. b) Reasons: It facilitates repair of worn out body tissues It leads to body building hence growth It brings about regeneration of parts of organisms e.g. in reptiles It is responsible of replacement of worn out cells It provides a basis for asexual reproduction It maintains genetic stability/the number of chromosomes in the nucleus is retained.",
      "24":"a) Diagram of osmosis set-up: Two solutions and a membrane Labels: e.g. water; salt or sugar solution; visking tubing or potato; beaker b) Control: e.g. Only water in visking tubing and in the beaker; only water around the semi permeable membrane. c) Result: The visking tubing increase in size if it contains a salt or sugar solution. Or water moves from hypotonic solution to hypertonic",
      "25":""
   },
   "sections":[
      {
         "name":"A",
         "description":"Multiple Choice, True/False, and Fill-in-the-Blank Questions",
         "questions":[
            {
               "questionNumber":1,
               "text":"Which of the following blood cells contain haemoglobin?",
               "type":"multiple-choice",
               "options":[
                  {"letter":"a", "text":"Lymphocytes", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"b", "text":"Phagocytes", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"c", "text":"Red blood cells", "isCorrect":true},
                  {"letter":"d", "text":"Platelets", "isCorrect":false}
               ],
               "correctAnswer":"c",
               "points":1
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":2,
               "text":"What is the function of the cornified layer of the epidermis of human skin?",
               "type":"multiple-choice",
               "options":[
                  {"letter":"a", "text":"Temperature regulation through sweating", "isCorrect":true},
                  {"letter":"b", "text":"Perception of sensation of temperature, pain and pressure", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"c", "text":"Synthesis of Vitamin D", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"d", "text":"Prevention of uncontrolled water loss by evaporation", "isCorrect":false}
               ],
               "correctAnswer":"a",
               "points":1
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":3,
               "text":"Which acid is produced in human muscle when there is no enough oxygen?",
               "type":"multiple-choice",
               "options":[
                  {"letter":"a", "text":"Lactic acid", "isCorrect":true},
                  {"letter":"b", "text":"Hydrochloric acid", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"c", "text":"Citric acid", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"d", "text":"Ascorbic acid", "isCorrect":false}
               ],
               "correctAnswer":"a",
               "points":1
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":4,
               "text":"Which of these statements below is not biologically correct?",
               "type":"multiple-choice",
               "options":[
                  {"letter":"a", "text":"All mammals are vertebrates", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"b", "text":"Frog's body is moist or rough skin", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"c", "text":"Birds have feathers", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"d", "text":"Amphibians have scales", "isCorrect":true}
               ],
               "correctAnswer":"d",
               "points":1
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":5,
               "text":"Which of these is not a function of joints?",
               "type":"multiple-choice",
               "options":[
                  {"letter":"a", "text":"Articulations", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"b", "text":"Allows bone growth", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"c", "text":"Enables mobility", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"d", "text":"Supports body", "isCorrect":true}
               ],
               "correctAnswer":"d",
               "points":1
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":6,
               "text":"Complete the following table with the corresponding type of contraceptive method: hormonal or non-hormonal or barrier.",
               "type":"fill-in-blank",
               "correctAnswer":"Method Type Spermicide Barrier accept also Non-Hormonal Condom Barrier Implant Hormonal IUD Non-hormonal also accept hormonal or barrier Pills Hormonal",
               "points":5
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":7,
               "text":"State whether each of the following statements is true or false.",
               "type":"true-false",
               "options":[
                  {"letter":"a", "text":"Mutations cannot be caused by copying errors in the genetic material during cell division.", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"b", "text":"Mutations can be caused by environmental changes.", "isCorrect":true},
                  {"letter":"c", "text":"Mutations can lead to death", "isCorrect":true},
                  {"letter":"d", "text":"Albinos are results of mutation in humans", "isCorrect":true},
                  {"letter":"e", "text":"Genotypic variations are visible", "isCorrect":false},
                  {"letter":"f", "text":"Phenotypic variations are visible", "isCorrect":true}
               ],
               "correctAnswer":"a) False b) True c) True d) True e) False f) True",
               "points":6
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":8,
               "text":"Fill the blank spaces in the paragraph below choosing word from the following list: meiosis, fertilization, parent cells, haploid, diploid, gametes.",
               "type":"fill-in-blank",
               "correctAnswer":"The process of meiosis produces unique reproductive cells called gametes which have half the number of chromosomes as the parent cell. During fertilization the fusion of gametes from two individuals, restores the diploid condition. Gametes are haploid (containing only one set of chromosomes) while the zygote is diploid (containing two sets of chromosomes). Thus, sexually reproducing organisms alternate between haploid and diploid stages. In sexual reproduction, two individuals produce diploid offsprings that have characteristics from both parents. The gametes are produced from parent cells via the process called meiosis.",
               "points":7
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":9,
               "text":"Match the following blood cells with their functions:",
               "type":"matching",
               "options":[
                  {"text":"White blood cells", "match":"Defend the body against pathogens"},
                  {"text":"Red blood cells", "match":"transport of respiratory gases"},
                  {"text":"platelets", "match":"Clotting of blood"}
               ],
               "correctAnswer":"White blood cells - Defend the body against pathogens, Red blood cells - transport of respiratory gases, platelets - Clotting of blood",
               "points":3
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":10,
               "text":"Write Yes if the statement is correct and No if it is wrong.",
               "type":"true-false",
               "options":[
                  {"letter":"a", "text":"ATP is released in the mitochondrion.", "isCorrect":true},
                  {"letter":"b", "text":"Aerobic respiration releases more energy than anaerobic respiration.", "isCorrect":true},
                  {"letter":"c", "text":"Cellular respiration occurs only in animal cells.", "isCorrect":false}
               ],
               "correctAnswer":"a) Yes b) Yes c) No",
               "points":3
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":11,
               "text":"Identify the parts of human reproductive system in which each of the following gametes is produced.",
               "type":"fill-in-blank",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Testis / testicle / male gonads b) Ovary /Ovaries / female gonads",
               "points":2
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":12,
               "text":"The age of four individuals is as follows: Individual 75-year-old grandfather B 35-year-old father 28-year-old pregnant mother D D: 3-year-old boy",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"a) D: 3-year-old boy. b) Milk contains important proteins which are very essential for the growth/Body building /development and repair of the body. Children have growing bodies than adults. Hence they are given more milk than grandfather who is adult. c) A 28 year old mother needs extra iron because:- Formation of more blood in both foetus and mother.- Formation of haemoglobin- Formation of red blood cells- Avoid anaemia-Transport of more oxygen",
               "points":3
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":13,
               "text":"Athletes are normally given glucose and not sucrose. Give a reason for this.",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"Glucose is a simple sugar/monosaccharide which can be broken down easily to produce energy. The Absorption of glucose is also very fast in the blood.- No need of its digestion",
               "points":3
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":14,
               "text":"What is the basic measure can you take to protect yourself from contracting a disease?",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"Protection/ keeping proper hygiene Keeping personal hygiene by washing your hands often with soap and clean water Using sanitizer, cleaning and disinfecting surfaces that are often touched Cover the food in cleaned utensils and avoid drinking non boiled water.",
               "points":2
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":15,
               "text":"Explain why farmers who grow cereals such as wheat, barley and rice must ensure that plants grow close to each other.",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"To allow cross pollination to take place casily. To ensure high yield production To ensure support each other against wind To allow maximum absorption of water/ Easy irrigation To maximize land use",
               "points":2
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":16,
               "text":"In your opinion, how would you deal with sexual assault?",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"Report to authorities, parents, teachers and guardians. Help the victim to avoid to be isolated Giving the victim medical assistance in case sexual assault is attempted/to take the victim to the ISANGE ONE STOP CENTER /Hospital / Health center. Avoiding peer pressure Counseling Avoid drugs abuse Education/ sensitization Consider legal actions Say no Gathering and keeping physical evidences",
               "points":2
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":17,
               "text":"Why should industrial plants treat their waste water before dumping in water bodies?",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"Industrial effluent should be treated for safe use on the land that is used as manure or for disposal, Industrial plants treat their waste water before dumping in water bodies to prevent pollution. For safe use on the land that is used as manure or for disposal To prevent pollution To prevent eutrophication Avoid acidification of water bodies Prevent water-borne diseases Production of biogas Avoid death of aquatic organisms For being reused.",
               "points":4
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":18,
               "text":"Fill in the blank spaces.",
               "type":"fill-in-blank",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Antigens bind at the active /antibody/ antigen-binding/binding site forming antigen-antibody complexes. b) Passive / active / adaptive immunity is a naturally acquired immunity.",
               "points":3
            }
         ]
      },
      {
         "name":"B",
         "description":"Short Answer Questions",
         "questions":[
            {
               "questionNumber":19,
               "text":"How can we help someone with cholera? List any four practices to ensure good health.",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Take the patient immediately to the hospital for treatment, Give the person rehydration salts/fuids Give the patient the Intravenous fluids and electrolyte. b) Daily practices to ensure good health include: i) Brushing our teeth three times in a day and every after a meal. ii) Practicing moderate physical exercises iii) Healthy eating: balanced diet, avoid too much fats/eating at close intervals iv) Vaccination and immunisation practices v) Regular medical check-up in hospitals vi) Sanitation i.e keeping our surroundings clean.",
               "points":10
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":20,
               "text":"Describe the negative impact of monoculture system to the biological diversity in an ecosystem.",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"Monoculture crops have no genetic variability because they are not allowed to reproduce naturally. This exposes their pests and weeds to environmental adaptations and become resistant to chemicals used to control them as the only way to control pests in this set up is to use more chemical control mechanisms Susceptibility to pests: The pests and weeds that attack them adapt easily to the environment and develop resistance to chemicals used to control them. Environment pollution through the use of dangerous synthetic chemicals/ Eutrophication May cause eutrophication that affect aquatic life Use of fuel energy in combination with the chemical fertilizers and pesticides, cause environment pollution (Air, soil and water) and lead to climate change May cause habitat destruction through searching enough space for growing crops for rising demand for food and other agricultural products Extinction of some species Soil erosion Use of dangerous synthetic chemicals It leads to soil degradation Drought Extreme weather condition Use of biofuels Conflict Food wastage",
               "points":10
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":21,
               "text":"Answer the following in relation to blood.",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Liquid part of blood b) Plasma transports blood cells, nutrients such as glucose and amino acids; transports antibodies, metabolic waste products... Transportation/ distribution of blood cells Transportation/ distribution of antibodies Transportation/ distribution of waste substances such as carbon dioxide and urea Transportation/ distribution of Hormones such as adrenaline, insulin and glucagon c) White blood cell: Protects the body against microorganisms. d) A or B or AB or O e) +/- factor: Rhesus factor or Rh factor f) For proper blood transfusion, to avoid incompatibility of blood/agglutination/ in case of blood transfusion / For Safe organ transplant. g) Platelets are responsible of blood clotting.",
               "points":10
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":22,
               "text":"A man whose blood group is A marries a woman whose blood group B. If one of their children is blood group O:",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Genotypes of the parents: AO and BO or I4 I° and I° b) Phenotype of parents: group A x group B Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I° Gametes: I*, I° and I*, I° The genotypes of their children are: 25% or = AB 25% or > BO 25% or AO 25% or + OO Or Phenotype of parents: group A x group B Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I° Gametes: I*, I° and I*, I° The genotypes of their children are: 25% or KB 25% or ~ I8 I° 25% or IA I° 10",
               "points":10
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":23,
               "text":"What do you understand by the term mitosis? Mitosis is essential in all living organisms. Give reasons to support your answer.",
               "type":"short-answer",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Mitosis: is a type of cell division in which a parent cell divides itself into two identical daughter cells which are genetically the same as the parent cell. b) Reasons: It facilitates repair of worn out body tissues It leads to body building hence growth It brings about regeneration of parts of organisms e.g. in reptiles It is responsible of replacement of worn out cells It provides a basis for asexual reproduction It maintains genetic stability/the number of chromosomes in the nucleus is retained.",
               "points":10
            }
         ]
      },
      {
         "name":"C",
         "description":"Essay/Long Answer Questions",
         "questions":[
            {
               "questionNumber":24,
               "text":"Assume you are performing an experiment to demonstrate osmosis.",
               "type":"essay",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Diagram of osmosis set-up: Two solutions and a membrane Labels: e.g. water; salt or sugar solution; visking tubing or potato; beaker b) Control: e.g. Only water in visking tubing and in the beaker; only water around the semi permeable membrane. c) Result: The visking tubing increase in size if it contains a salt or sugar solution. Or water moves from hypotonic solution to hypertonic",
               "points":15
            }
         ]
      }
   ]
}

parsedContent.instructions: 1) Write your names and index number on the answer booklet as they appear on your registration form and DO NOT write your names and index number on additional answer sheets of paper if provided. 2) Do not open this paper until you are told to do so, 3) This paper consists of three sections A, B and C Section A: Answer ALL questions. (55 marks) Section B: Answer THREE questions. (30 marks) SECTION C: This section is compulsory. (15 marks) 4) Use only a blue or black pen.

answerKey.6: Method Type Spermicide Barrier accept also Non-Hormonal Condom Barrier Implant Hormonal IUD Non-hormonal also accept hormonal or barrier Pills Hormonal
answerKey.7: a) False b) True c) True d) True e) False f) True
answerKey.8: The process of meiosis produces unique reproductive cells called gametes which have half the number of chromosomes as the parent cell. During fertilization the fusion of gametes from two individuals, restores the diploid condition. Gametes are haploid (containing only one set of chromosomes) while the zygote is diploid (containing two sets of chromosomes). Thus, sexually reproducing organisms alternate between haploid and diploid stages. In sexual reproduction, two individuals produce diploid offsprings that have characteristics from both parents. The gametes are produced from parent cells via the process called meiosis.
answerKey.9: Blood component function White blood cells Defend the body against pathogens Red blood cells transport of respiratory gases platelets Clotting of blood
answerKey.10: a) Yes b) Yes c) No
answerKey.11: a) Testis / testicle / male gonads b) Ovary /Ovaries / female gonads
answerKey.12: a) D: 3-year-old boy. b) Milk contains important proteins which are very essential for the growth/Body building /development and repair of the body. Children have growing bodies than adults. Hence they are given more milk than grandfather who is adult. c) A 28 year old mother needs extra iron because:- Formation of more blood in both foetus and mother.- Formation of haemoglobin- Formation of red blood cells- Avoid anaemia-Transport of more oxygen
answerKey.13: Glucose is a simple sugar/monosaccharide which can be broken down easily to produce energy. The Absorption of glucose is also very fast in the blood.- No need of its digestion
answerKey.14: Protection/ keeping proper hygiene Keeping personal hygiene by washing your hands often with soap and clean water Using sanitizer, cleaning and disinfecting surfaces that are often touched Cover the food in cleaned utensils and avoid drinking non boiled water.
answerKey.15: To allow cross pollination to take place casily. To ensure high yield production To ensure support each other against wind To allow maximum absorption of water/ Easy irrigation To maximize land use
answerKey.16: Report to authorities, parents, teachers and guardians. Help the victim to avoid to be isolated Giving the victim medical assistance in case sexual assault is attempted/to take the victim to the ISANGE ONE STOP CENTER /Hospital / Health center. Avoiding peer pressure Counseling Avoid drugs abuse Education/ sensitization Consider legal actions Say no Gathering and keeping physical evidences
answerKey.17: Industrial effluent should be treated for safe use on the land that is used as manure or for disposal, Industrial plants treat their waste water before dumping in water bodies to prevent pollution. For safe use on the land that is used as manure or for disposal To prevent pollution To prevent eutrophication Avoid acidification of water bodies Prevent water-borne diseases Production of biogas Avoid death of aquatic organisms For being reused.
answerKey.18: a) Antigens bind at the active /antibody/ antigen-binding/binding site forming antigen-antibody complexes. b) Passive / active / adaptive immunity is a naturally acquired immunity.
answerKey.19: a) Take the patient immediately to the hospital for treatment, Give the person rehydration salts/fuids Give the patient the Intravenous fluids and electrolyte. b) Daily practices to ensure good health include: i) Brushing our teeth three times in a day and every after a meal. ii) Practicing moderate physical exercises iii) Healthy eating: balanced diet, avoid too much fats/eating at close intervals iv) Vaccination and immunisation practices v) Regular medical check-up in hospitals vi) Sanitation i.e keeping our surroundings clean.
answerKey.20: Monoculture crops have no genetic variability because they are not allowed to reproduce naturally. This exposes their pests and weeds to environmental adaptations and become resistant to chemicals used to control them as the only way to control pests in this set up is to use more chemical control mechanisms Susceptibility to pests: The pests and weeds that attack them adapt easily to the environment and develop resistance to chemicals used to control them. Environment pollution through the use of dangerous synthetic chemicals/ Eutrophication May cause eutrophication that affect aquatic life Use of fuel energy in combination with the chemical fertilizers and pesticides, cause environment pollution (Air, soil and water) and lead to climate change May cause habitat destruction through searching enough space for growing crops for rising demand for food and other agricultural products Extinction of some species Soil erosion Use of dangerous synthetic chemicals It leads to soil degradation Drought Extreme weather condition Use of biofuels Conflict Food wastage
answerKey.21: a) Liquid part of blood b) Plasma transports blood cells, nutrients such as glucose and amino acids; transports antibodies, metabolic waste products... Transportation/ distribution of blood cells Transportation/ distribution of antibodies Transportation/ distribution of waste substances such as carbon dioxide and urea Transportation/ distribution of Hormones such as adrenaline, insulin and glucagon c) White blood cell: Protects the body against microorganisms. d) A or B or AB or O e) +/- factor: Rhesus factor or Rh factor f) For proper blood transfusion, to avoid incompatibility of blood/agglutination/ in case of blood transfusion / For Safe organ transplant. g) Platelets are responsible of blood clotting.
answerKey.22: a) Genotypes of the parents: AO and BO or I4 I° and I° b) Phenotype of parents: group A x group B Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I° Gametes: I*, I° and I*, I° The genotypes of their children are: 25% or = AB 25% or > BO 25% or AO 25% or + OO Or Phenotype of parents: group A x group B Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I° Gametes: I*, I° and I*, I° The genotypes of their children are: 25% or KB 25% or ~ I8 I° 25% or IA I° 10
answerKey.23: a) Mitosis: is a type of cell division in which a parent cell divides itself into two identical daughter cells which are genetically the same as the parent cell. b) Reasons: It facilitates repair of worn out body tissues It leads to body building hence growth It brings about regeneration of parts of organisms e.g. in reptiles It is responsible of replacement of worn out cells It provides a basis for asexual reproduction It maintains genetic stability/the number of chromosomes in the nucleus is retained.
answerKey.24: a) Diagram of osmosis set-up: Two solutions and a membrane Labels: e.g. water; salt or sugar solution; visking tubing or potato; beaker b) Control: e.g. Only water in visking tubing and in the beaker; only water around the semi permeable membrane. c) Result: The visking tubing increase in size if it contains a salt or sugar solution. Or water moves from hypotonic solution to hypertonic

sections.name: A
sections.description: Multiple Choice, True/False, and Fill-in-the-Blank Questions

questions.text: Which of the following blood cells contain haemoglobin?
questions.correctAnswer: c

questions.text: What is the function of the cornified layer of the epidermis of human skin?

options.text: Perception of sensation of temperature, pain and pressure
Prevention of uncontrolled water loss by evaporation

questions.correctAnswer: a

questions.text: Which acid is produced in human muscle when there is no enough oxygen?
questions.correctAnswer: a

questions.text: Which of these statements below is not biologically correct?

options.text: Frog's body is moist or rough skin

questions.correctAnswer: d

questions.text: Which of these is not a function of joints?
questions.correctAnswer: d

questions.text: Complete the following table with the corresponding type of contraceptive method: hormonal or non-hormonal or barrier.
questions.correctAnswer: Method Type Spermicide Barrier accept also Non-Hormonal Condom Barrier Implant Hormonal IUD Non-hormonal also accept hormonal or barrier Pills Hormonal

questions.text: State whether each of the following statements is true or false.

options.text: Mutations cannot be caused by copying errors in the genetic material during cell division.
Mutations can be caused by environmental changes.
Albinos are results of mutation in humans

questions.correctAnswer: a) False b) True c) True d) True e) False f) True

questions.text: Fill the blank spaces in the paragraph below choosing word from the following list: meiosis, fertilization, parent cells, haploid, diploid, gametes.
questions.correctAnswer: The process of meiosis produces unique reproductive cells called gametes which have half the number of chromosomes as the parent cell. During fertilization the fusion of gametes from two individuals, restores the diploid condition. Gametes are haploid (containing only one set of chromosomes) while the zygote is diploid (containing two sets of chromosomes). Thus, sexually reproducing organisms alternate between haploid and diploid stages. In sexual reproduction, two individuals produce diploid offsprings that have characteristics from both parents. The gametes are produced from parent cells via the process called meiosis.

questions.text: Match the following blood cells with their functions:
questions.correctAnswer: White blood cells - Defend the body against pathogens, Red blood cells - transport of respiratory gases, platelets - Clotting of blood

questions.text: Write Yes if the statement is correct and No if it is wrong.

options.text: ATP is released in the mitochondrion.
Aerobic respiration releases more energy than anaerobic respiration.
Cellular respiration occurs only in animal cells.

questions.correctAnswer: a) Yes b) Yes c) No

questions.text: Identify the parts of human reproductive system in which each of the following gametes is produced.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Testis / testicle / male gonads b) Ovary /Ovaries / female gonads

questions.text: The age of four individuals is as follows: Individual 75-year-old grandfather B 35-year-old father 28-year-old pregnant mother D D: 3-year-old boy
questions.correctAnswer: a) D: 3-year-old boy. b) Milk contains important proteins which are very essential for the growth/Body building /development and repair of the body. Children have growing bodies than adults. Hence they are given more milk than grandfather who is adult. c) A 28 year old mother needs extra iron because:- Formation of more blood in both foetus and mother.- Formation of haemoglobin- Formation of red blood cells- Avoid anaemia-Transport of more oxygen

questions.text: Athletes are normally given glucose and not sucrose. Give a reason for this.
questions.correctAnswer: Glucose is a simple sugar/monosaccharide which can be broken down easily to produce energy. The Absorption of glucose is also very fast in the blood.- No need of its digestion

questions.text: What is the basic measure can you take to protect yourself from contracting a disease?
questions.correctAnswer: Protection/ keeping proper hygiene Keeping personal hygiene by washing your hands often with soap and clean water Using sanitizer, cleaning and disinfecting surfaces that are often touched Cover the food in cleaned utensils and avoid drinking non boiled water.

questions.text: Explain why farmers who grow cereals such as wheat, barley and rice must ensure that plants grow close to each other.
questions.correctAnswer: To allow cross pollination to take place casily. To ensure high yield production To ensure support each other against wind To allow maximum absorption of water/ Easy irrigation To maximize land use

questions.text: In your opinion, how would you deal with sexual assault?
questions.correctAnswer: Report to authorities, parents, teachers and guardians. Help the victim to avoid to be isolated Giving the victim medical assistance in case sexual assault is attempted/to take the victim to the ISANGE ONE STOP CENTER /Hospital / Health center. Avoiding peer pressure Counseling Avoid drugs abuse Education/ sensitization Consider legal actions Say no Gathering and keeping physical evidences

questions.text: Why should industrial plants treat their waste water before dumping in water bodies?
questions.correctAnswer: Industrial effluent should be treated for safe use on the land that is used as manure or for disposal, Industrial plants treat their waste water before dumping in water bodies to prevent pollution. For safe use on the land that is used as manure or for disposal To prevent pollution To prevent eutrophication Avoid acidification of water bodies Prevent water-borne diseases Production of biogas Avoid death of aquatic organisms For being reused.

questions.text: Fill in the blank spaces.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Antigens bind at the active /antibody/ antigen-binding/binding site forming antigen-antibody complexes. b) Passive / active / adaptive immunity is a naturally acquired immunity.

questions.text: How can we help someone with cholera? List any four practices to ensure good health.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Take the patient immediately to the hospital for treatment, Give the person rehydration salts/fuids Give the patient the Intravenous fluids and electrolyte. b) Daily practices to ensure good health include: i) Brushing our teeth three times in a day and every after a meal. ii) Practicing moderate physical exercises iii) Healthy eating: balanced diet, avoid too much fats/eating at close intervals iv) Vaccination and immunisation practices v) Regular medical check-up in hospitals vi) Sanitation i.e keeping our surroundings clean.

questions.text: Describe the negative impact of monoculture system to the biological diversity in an ecosystem.
questions.correctAnswer: Monoculture crops have no genetic variability because they are not allowed to reproduce naturally. This exposes their pests and weeds to environmental adaptations and become resistant to chemicals used to control them as the only way to control pests in this set up is to use more chemical control mechanisms Susceptibility to pests: The pests and weeds that attack them adapt easily to the environment and develop resistance to chemicals used to control them. Environment pollution through the use of dangerous synthetic chemicals/ Eutrophication May cause eutrophication that affect aquatic life Use of fuel energy in combination with the chemical fertilizers and pesticides, cause environment pollution (Air, soil and water) and lead to climate change May cause habitat destruction through searching enough space for growing crops for rising demand for food and other agricultural products Extinction of some species Soil erosion Use of dangerous synthetic chemicals It leads to soil degradation Drought Extreme weather condition Use of biofuels Conflict Food wastage

questions.text: Answer the following in relation to blood.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Liquid part of blood b) Plasma transports blood cells, nutrients such as glucose and amino acids; transports antibodies, metabolic waste products... Transportation/ distribution of blood cells Transportation/ distribution of antibodies Transportation/ distribution of waste substances such as carbon dioxide and urea Transportation/ distribution of Hormones such as adrenaline, insulin and glucagon c) White blood cell: Protects the body against microorganisms. d) A or B or AB or O e) +/- factor: Rhesus factor or Rh factor f) For proper blood transfusion, to avoid incompatibility of blood/agglutination/ in case of blood transfusion / For Safe organ transplant. g) Platelets are responsible of blood clotting.

questions.text: A man whose blood group is A marries a woman whose blood group B. If one of their children is blood group O:
questions.correctAnswer: a) Genotypes of the parents: AO and BO or I4 I° and I° b) Phenotype of parents: group A x group B Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I° Gametes: I*, I° and I*, I° The genotypes of their children are: 25% or = AB 25% or > BO 25% or AO 25% or + OO Or Phenotype of parents: group A x group B Genotype of parents: I* I9 x I* I° Gametes: I*, I° and I*, I° The genotypes of their children are: 25% or KB 25% or ~ I8 I° 25% or IA I° 10

questions.text: What do you understand by the term mitosis? Mitosis is essential in all living organisms. Give reasons to support your answer.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Mitosis: is a type of cell division in which a parent cell divides itself into two identical daughter cells which are genetically the same as the parent cell. b) Reasons: It facilitates repair of worn out body tissues It leads to body building hence growth It brings about regeneration of parts of organisms e.g. in reptiles It is responsible of replacement of worn out cells It provides a basis for asexual reproduction It maintains genetic stability/the number of chromosomes in the nucleus is retained.

questions.text: Assume you are performing an experiment to demonstrate osmosis.
questions.correctAnswer: a) Diagram of osmosis set-up: Two solutions and a membrane Labels: e.g. water; salt or sugar solution; visking tubing or potato; beaker b) Control: e.g. Only water in visking tubing and in the beaker; only water around the semi permeable membrane. c) Result: The visking tubing increase in size if it contains a salt or sugar solution. Or water moves from hypotonic solution to hypertonic
